---

title: "The Link Between Vaccination and Child Survival"
author: "Your Name"
format:
html:
embed-resources: true
code-fold: true
theme: cosmo
------------

## 🌍 Global Health Inequality

Vaccination is one of the most effective public health interventions in the world. However, access remains uneven across countries.

This report explores the relationship between **Vitamin A supplementation (a proxy for vaccination)** and **child mortality rates**, highlighting how improved health interventions can save lives.

---

## 📊 Data Preparation

```{python}
#| label: data-prep
#| echo: true

import polars as pl

df1 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_1.csv")
df2 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_2.csv")

# Vaccination (Vitamin A)
vaccination = df1.select([
    "country",
    "time_period",
    "obs_value"
]).rename({
    "obs_value": "vaccination_rate"
})

# Mortality
mortality = df2.select([
    "country",
    "time_period",
    "obs_value"
]).rename({
    "obs_value": "mortality_rate"
})

# Merge datasets
merged = vaccination.join(
    mortality,
    on=["country", "time_period"],
    how="inner"
).drop_nulls()

# Latest year
latest_year = merged.select(pl.col("time_period").max()).item()
latest_data = merged.filter(pl.col("time_period") == latest_year)
```

---

## 📈 Top Countries by Mortality

```{python}
#| label: bar-chart
#| fig-cap: "Top 10 countries with highest child mortality"

from plotnine import *

top10 = latest_data.sort("mortality_rate", descending=True).head(10)

(
    ggplot(top10, aes(x="reorder(country, mortality_rate)", y="mortality_rate"))
    + geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="red")
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title="Top 10 Countries with Highest Child Mortality",
        x="Country",
        y="Mortality Rate"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

👉 Countries with the highest mortality rates highlight where urgent intervention is needed.

---

## 🔍 Vaccination vs Mortality

```{python}
#| label: scatter
#| fig-cap: "Relationship between vaccination and mortality"

(
    ggplot(merged, aes(x="vaccination_rate", y="mortality_rate"))
    + geom_point(alpha=0.5, color="blue")
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="red")
    + labs(
        title="Vaccination vs Child Mortality",
        x="Vaccination Rate (Vitamin A)",
        y="Mortality Rate"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

The scatterplot reveals a **negative relationship** — higher vaccination rates are associated with lower child mortality.

---

## ⏳ Mortality Trends Over Time

```{python}
#| label: time-series
#| fig-cap: "Global mortality trend over time"

time_data = merged.group_by("time_period").agg([
    pl.mean("mortality_rate").alias("avg_mortality")
])

(
    ggplot(time_data, aes(x="time_period", y="avg_mortality"))
    + geom_line(color="red")
    + labs(
        title="Global Trend in Child Mortality",
        x="Year",
        y="Average Mortality Rate"
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

Over time, mortality rates have generally declined, reflecting improvements in healthcare access.

---

## 🌎 Global Distribution of Mortality

```{python}
#| label: world-map
#| fig-cap: "Global distribution of child mortality"

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd

url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector/master/geojson/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson"
world = gpd.read_file(url)

map_data = latest_data.to_pandas()

world_data = world.merge(map_data, how="left", left_on="ADMIN", right_on="country")

world_data.plot(column="mortality_rate", legend=True, cmap="Reds")
```

The map highlights geographic disparities, with some regions facing significantly higher mortality rates.

---

## 📌 Key Insights

* Countries with higher vaccination rates tend to have lower mortality
* Significant inequality exists across regions
* Progress has been made, but gaps remain

---

## ✅ Conclusion

Improving vaccination coverage is critical to reducing child mortality worldwide. Targeted policies and global cooperation are essential to ensure no child is left behind.
